name: ollama-codex
description: An in-Codex visual control panel and command layer for configuring Codex App and Codex CLI with Ollama.

interface.shortDescription: Use a Codex Mac app model switcher for Ollama options.
interface.longDescription: Ollama can already work with Codex, but discovery is low and the command surface is tedious to remember. Ollama for Codex gives Ollama a visual, reversible home inside the Codex Mac app: an MCP-powered model switcher and control panel, starter prompts, slash commands, status checks, dry runs, model helpers, and explicit restore flows. Open `/ollama-codex-panel` to render the panel in chat, see a Codex/OpenAI lane and an Ollama lane at the same time, scan the actual Codex/OpenAI model catalog beside Ollama options, see the active Codex/OpenAI profile and Ollama App model side by side, filter recommended and local choices, choose from Ollama's recommended Codex models, local models, or typed cloud tags, pull a model, configure Codex App, configure Codex CLI, and restore either profile. When Ollama is active, the panel shows the previous native Codex profile when a backup is available and restores that profile instead of hand-editing OpenAI state. Recommended and local rows are deduplicated, counted, and badged when a recommended model is already installed. Active and configured badges make it clear which profile is live before switching. A model row's Switch button switches Codex App to that model; setup, restore, pull, and CLI buttons are explicit local-state actions from the panel. Direct non-dry-run MCP calls still require confirmation from the caller. For Codex App, the backend uses `ollama launch codex-app --yes`, switches to a local or Ollama Cloud model with `ollama launch codex-app --model <model> --yes`, and restores the previous App profile with `ollama launch codex-app --restore --yes`. For Codex CLI, it can create a non-launching `ollama-launch` profile for a chosen model, restore via `ollama launch codex --restore`, prepare terminal commands for `codex --oss`, use a specific local or cloud model with `codex --oss -m <model>`, or use the generated `ollama-launch` profile with `codex --profile ollama-launch`. The plugin preserves App/CLI profile separation and delegates Codex App configuration to Ollama.
defaultPrompt: Switch Codex App to an Ollama model
